_note: Slab c.1550-1400 BC, two threads. (1) CHINA — the notable EARLY & MIDDLE SHANG kings that the existing bridge CN_SHANGMID glosses over, added as a PARALLEL detail king-list hanging off the existing CN_TAIJIA (Tai Jia). This chain runs Wai Bing -> Zhong Ren -> Tai Geng -> Xiao Jia -> Yong Ji -> Tai Wu -> Zhong Ding -> Wai Ren -> He Dan Jia -> Zu Yi -> Zu Xin -> Wo Jia -> Zu Ding -> Nan Geng and then STOPS at Nan Geng: it deliberately does NOT reconnect to the existing later king Wu Ding (CN_WUDING), because Wu Ding already has a parent via the CN_SHANGMID bridge and must not be re-parented. These reigns would slot between Tai Jia (c.1550) and Wu Ding (c.1250); the intervening Wo Ding, Yang Jia, Pan Geng, Xiao Xin & Xiao Yi are left to the existing bridge. Status flagged honestly: the earliest of these (Wai Bing through Yong Ji) are SEMI-LEGENDARY traditional kings; from Tai Wu onward the dynasty is treated as HISTORICAL, its kings named on the oracle-bone sacrificial records (individual pre-Wu-Ding dates remain approximate/reconstructed). Order follows the Shiji king-list as cross-checked against oracle bones (Wikipedia 'Shang dynasty'); prefixed CN_. (2) THE AEGEAN — the START of the legendary royal genealogies, ALL clearly flagged LEGENDARY / mythological, with NO claim to historicity: CRETE (Minoan, prefix CRE_) around Minos of Knossos, and MYCENAE/ARGOS (Mycenaean, prefix MYC_) around Danaus and Perseus. Zeus is marked a mythological deity. These are new houses seeded by prefix. Verified against Wikipedia/Britannica entries for Minos, Europa, Pasiphae, Perseus, Andromeda and the Perseids. No fabrication: only figures, relations and roles found in the received tradition. Child years are drawn smaller than the parent's to keep the timeline ordered; mythological dates are nominal placeholders, not historical claims.

families.note: CHINA thread — parallel early/middle Shang king-list hung off the EXISTING Tai Jia (CN_TAIJIA). CN_TAIJIA is only used as a parent here; no existing CN_ king is placed in any children array.

children.label:
👑 Wai Bing
c.1548 BC · early Shang king; younger brother of Tai Ding, reigned briefly after Tang's line · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Zhong Ren
c.1546 BC · early Shang king; a short reign; brother-successor in the Shiji king-list · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Tai Geng
c.1540 BC · early Shang king; son of Tai Jia in the Shiji annals · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Xiao Jia
c.1530 BC · early Shang king; recorded as son or younger brother of Tai Geng · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Yong Ji
c.1518 BC · early Shang king; reigned before Tai Wu in the Historical Records · EARLY SHANG, SEMI-LEGENDARY

👑 Tai Wu
c.1500 BC · long-reigning Shang king (traditionally ~75 years); under him the dynasty flourished · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Zhong Ding
c.1465 BC · Shang king; the Shiji records him moving the capital to Ao · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Wai Ren
c.1455 BC · Shang king; brother-successor of Zhong Ding · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 He Dan Jia
c.1445 BC · Shang king; a period of decline in the traditional accounts · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Zu Yi
c.1435 BC · Shang king; credited with reviving Shang fortunes & moving the capital to Xing · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Zu Xin
c.1420 BC · Shang king; son of Zu Yi in the king-list · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Wo Jia (Qiang Jia)
c.1410 BC · Shang king; brother-successor of Zu Xin · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Zu Ding
c.1400 BC · Shang king; son of Zu Xin in the king-list · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

👑 Nan Geng
c.1385 BC · Shang king; his successors (Yang Jia, Pan Geng, Xiao Xin, Xiao Yi) lead on toward Wu Ding via the existing bridge · SHANG, HISTORICAL (oracle-bone attested)

families.note: AEGEAN thread — CRETE (Minoan). Agenor of Tyre, father of Europa; Phoenician by origin but grouped with the Aegean legendary set by the CRE_ prefix. ALL figures below are LEGENDARY / mythological.

a.label:
👑 Agenor
c.1520 BC · legendary Phoenician king of Tyre; father of Europa · LEGENDARY

children.label:
Europa
c.1490 BC · Phoenician princess of Tyre; carried to Crete by Zeus; mother of Minos · LEGENDARY

families.note: Zeus (mythological deity) & Europa, parents of the three Cretan brothers Minos, Rhadamanthus & Sarpedon. Europa is referenced from the family above.

a.label:
Zeus
c.1500 BC · king of the Greek gods; MYTHOLOGICAL DEITY; father of Minos by Europa · LEGENDARY

children.label:
👑 Minos
c.1450 BC · legendary king & lawgiver of Knossos, Crete; later a judge of the dead · LEGENDARY

👑 Rhadamanthus
c.1448 BC · brother of Minos; wise lawgiver & later a judge of the dead · LEGENDARY

👑 Sarpedon
c.1446 BC · brother of Minos; quarrelled with him & became king of Lycia · LEGENDARY

families.note: Minos & Pasiphae (daughter of Helios), parents of the Cretan royal children.

b.label:
Pasiphae
c.1445 BC · wife of Minos; daughter of the sun god Helios; mother of the Cretan royal children · LEGENDARY

children.label:
Ariadne
c.1420 BC · princess of Crete; gave Theseus the thread out of the Labyrinth · LEGENDARY

Phaedra
c.1418 BC · princess of Crete; later queen of Athens as wife of Theseus · LEGENDARY

Androgeus
c.1420 BC · prince of Crete; his death at Athens sparked the tribute of the Minotaur · LEGENDARY

👑 Deucalion
c.1416 BC · son of Minos; succeeded as king of Crete · LEGENDARY

👑 Catreus
c.1414 BC · son of Minos; king of Crete, fated to die by his own child's hand · LEGENDARY

families.note: AEGEAN thread — MYCENAE / ARGOS (Mycenaean). Danaus, ancestor of the Danaids; kept SEPARATE from Perseus per the received Argive tradition. ALL figures LEGENDARY.

a.label:
👑 Danaus
c.1500 BC · legendary king of Argos; fled Egypt with his fifty daughters, the Danaids · LEGENDARY

children.label:
the fifty Danaids
c.1470 BC · the fifty daughters of Danaus; from them the Argives took the name Danaans · LEGENDARY

families.note: Perseus & Andromeda, founders of the Perseid line that leads on toward Heracles & the Mycenaean kings. Perseus is a root here (his descent from Danae is not drawn).

a.label:
👑 Perseus
c.1400 BC · legendary hero, slayer of Medusa; founder-king of Mycenae · LEGENDARY

b.label:
Andromeda
c.1398 BC · Aethiopian princess rescued by Perseus; matriarch of the Perseids · LEGENDARY

children.label:
Perses
c.1375 BC · son of Perseus; left in Aethiopia, reckoned ancestor of the Persians · LEGENDARY

Alcaeus
c.1375 BC · son of Perseus; father of Amphitryon, in the line toward Heracles · LEGENDARY

👑 Electryon
c.1373 BC · son of Perseus; king of Mycenae; father of Alcmene, mother of Heracles · LEGENDARY

👑 Sthenelus
c.1371 BC · son of Perseus; king of Mycenae; father of Eurystheus · LEGENDARY
